prerequisites: A Microsoft Advertising account. If you do not have one, sign up via the Microsoft Advertising web application at https://ads.microsoft.com/.

A Bing Ads developer token (developer_token). Request one with Super Admin credentials from the Microsoft Advertising Developer Portal. A universal developer token can authenticate any Microsoft Advertising user.
An application registered in an Azure Active Directory (Microsoft Entra ID) tenant to obtain the Application (client) ID (client_id) and, for web/confidential apps, a client secret (client_secret).
An OAuth 2.0 refresh token (refresh_token) obtained after the Microsoft Advertising user grants consent to your registered application.
Access to the Microsoft Advertising accounts you want to sync. The accounts available through the API are the same ones available to the user in the Microsoft Advertising web application.

authMethods.name: OAuth 2.0 (Microsoft Entra ID) with developer token
authMethods.summary: Microsoft Advertising APIs use Azure Entra ID (formerly Azure AD) and the OAuth 2.0 protocol for authentication. The connector authenticates with client_id and (for confidential apps) client_secret from an Azure app registration, a refresh_token obtained after user consent, and a developer_token that grants programmatic API access. The optional tenant_id targets a specific Azure tenant. The developer token is sent in the DeveloperToken SOAP header and the OAuth access token (derived from the refresh token) in the AuthenticationToken header.

setupSteps.title: Get a developer token
setupSteps.body: Sign in with Super Admin credentials at the Microsoft Advertising Developer Portal account tab (https://ads.microsoft.com/cc/Settings/DevSettings), choose the user to associate with the token, and click Request Token. The resulting universal developer token can be used to authenticate with any Microsoft Advertising user credentials. Set this value as developer_token in the connector. For the sandbox environment, the universal sandbox developer token is BBD37VB98.

setupSteps.title: Register an application in Azure
setupSteps.body: In the Azure portal App registrations page, select New registration, enter a name, and under Supported account types select 'Any Entra ID Tenant + Personal Microsoft accounts'. After registering, copy the Application (client) ID from the Overview page and use it as the connector's client_id.

setupSteps.title: Configure a redirect URI and (for web apps) a client secret
setupSteps.body: Add a Redirect URI: for public/desktop clients select https://login.microsoftonline.com/common/oauth2/nativeclient; for web applications provide your application base URL. For web/confidential applications, go to Certificates & secrets, select New client secret, and copy the secret value to use as the connector's client_secret. A client secret is required to use a refresh token across devices and services.

setupSteps.title: Grant user consent and obtain a refresh token
setupSteps.body: Use the Microsoft authorization URL to prompt the Microsoft Advertising user for consent for your application to manage their accounts, then exchange the returned authorization code for access and refresh tokens. Supply the resulting refresh_token to the connector. Optionally set tenant_id to target a specific Azure tenant. Note: multi-factor authentication is required for Bing Ads.

setupSteps.title: Configure connector options
setupSteps.body: Optionally set reports_start_date to control how far back report data is pulled, lookback_window for attribution re-sync, account_names to limit which accounts are synced, custom_reports for additional report definitions, and num_workers to tune parallelism. Set auth_method to match your chosen identity provider; Microsoft Entra ID is supported and Google OAuth 2.0 is also supported as an alternative provider.
